issue: [{"role": "user", "content": "Hi, please pause our subscription. We have stopped all hiring efforts for now."}]
subject: Subscription pause
response: You can pause a DevPlatform subscription from the subscription settings. For DevPlatform for Work, go to Administration > Subscription, click "Pause Subscription," choose a pause duration of 30, 60, or 90 days, and confirm. During the pause, existing tests and candidate data are preserved, billing is suspended, but you won’t be able to create new tests or send new invitations. Existing active tests will complete normally, and the subscription will auto-resume at the end of the pause period or you can resume earlier from Administration > Subscription > "Resume Now." Source: DevPlatform Subscription Management — Quick Reference.

subject: Bug bounty
response: Thanks for reporting this. For security vulnerabilities in Claude products, please follow Anthropic’s responsible disclosure process and submit the report to security@anthropic.com with a detailed description, steps to reproduce, potential impact, and any proof-of-concept code. Anthropic’s bug bounty scope includes Claude web, APIs, mobile apps, Claude Code/Desktop, authentication/authorization, and data handling/privacy mechanisms. For universal jailbreaks related to ASL-3 biological-threat information, Anthropic provides a public reporting form, and the Model Safety Bug Bounty Program is run through HackerOne. Sources: Public Vulnerability Reporting; Claude API Security and Compliance — Enterprise Reference; Model Safety Bug Bounty Program.

issue: [{"role": "user", "content": "I want Claude to stop crawling by website"}]
subject: Website Data crawl
response: You can stop Claude’s web crawlers by adding robots.txt directives for ClaudeBot on your site. For a full site block, add a robots.txt rule such as "User-agent: ClaudeBot" and "Disallow: /". Anthropic’s web features respect robots.txt directives, and Crawl-delay is also supported if you want to limit rather than fully block crawling. If you prefer domain-level blocking or believe a bot is malfunctioning, you can contact crawler-control@anthropic.com or claudebot@anthropic.com. Sources: data/claude/privacy-and-legal/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler.md | data/claude/team-and-enterprise-plans/security-and-compliance/claude-api-security-compliance-enterprise.md

issue: [{"role": "user", "content": "I am facing multiple issues in my project. all requests to claude with aws bedrock is failing"}]
subject: Issues in Project
response:
If all requests to Claude through AWS Bedrock are failing, a few common checks from the Claude documentation may help:

- Verify your AWS credentials are working by confirming `aws sts get-caller-identity` succeeds.
- Confirm `AWS_REGION` is set to a region where the Claude model is enabled.
- If you see a 403 or “model not available,” your account may not have access to the specific model in that region. As a workaround, use `/model` and select a model you know you have access to.
- If this is happening in Claude Code, run `claude doctor` from your normal shell and attach the diagnostic report to your support request.

If you are using Claude through Amazon Bedrock, AWS Support or your AWS account manager is the correct support contact for Bedrock support inquiries. Anthropic’s support article also notes that Bedrock usage is non-refundable unless you have a private offer and direct contract with Anthropic. Sources: troubleshoot Claude Code installation/authentication; Amazon Bedrock support contact article.
justification: The ticket is a straightforward technical support issue about Claude requests failing via AWS Bedrock. The corpus directly covers common Bedrock credential and region troubleshooting steps, model access issues, and the correct support channel for Bedrock. No adversarial content or sensitive action request is present. Replying is appropriate because this is a simple guidance case grounded in the provided documentation.

If your card was retained by an ATM, contact your issuing bank immediately, note the ATM location, bank name, and time, and request help with a temporary card. To help avoid future travel-related blocks, Visa recommends setting up travel notifications through your bank. Sources: data/visa/support.md, data/claude/team-and-enterprise-plans/visa-emergency-travel-services.md
